name: Carpatho-Ukraine (1939)

colors.symbolism: the Carpathian sky and the heraldic azure of Ukrainian tradition
the wheat fields and the heraldic gold of Ukrainian tradition

description: A horizontal bicolour — blue over yellow — identical in design to the modern Ukrainian national flag.
history: Carpatho-Ukraine (Karpatska Ukraina) was an autonomous region of Czechoslovakia that declared independence on 15 March 1939, the same day Nazi Germany dismembered Czechoslovakia. The new republic adopted the blue-and-yellow Ukrainian national flag and survived just one day before being invaded and annexed by Hungary on 16 March 1939; sporadic resistance by the Carpathian Sich militia continued until 23 March. Despite its brevity, Carpatho-Ukraine is recognised as a key moment in 20th-century Ukrainian statehood.
